envelopesguide-text:
Envelopes are a way to send letters to other players. For sending items, use a parcel instead.
To send a letter, write it on a piece of parchment, craft an empty envelope, and combine them in the crafting grid. For a parcel, craft an empty parcel. Combine it with a piece of parchment in the crafting grid to put the letter inside. To send any item, place the parcel on a surface, crouch right click on it with the item in hand, then crouch and sprint right click it with a piece of parchment to wrap it up.

Wax sealing
To seal an envelope or parcel, you need a stick of wax and an engraved wax seal stamp. Make wax sticks by combining a bowl with some dye (green, blue, black, or red) with a piece of beeswax. Right click a blank stamp to engrave it and give it a name. Then put the wax stick, stamp, and envelope or parcel into the crafting grid to seal it. You can also seal in the world by holding the stamp with a wax stick in your offhand and right clicking the unsealed envelope or parcel on a surface.

Addressing
With an ink and quill in your offhand and an envelope or parcel in hand, hold sprint and right click to fill in the From and To fields.

Opening
Right click a sealed envelope to break the seal and retrieve the letter. For a parcel, right clicking breaks the seal but you then need to take the item out manually.

Wax scraping
To reuse an envelope or parcel, scrape off the wax with a knife. The wax is destroyed, but the envelope or parcel stays intact.
